issues_found: 77% of organizations reported breaches involving AI systems
29.1% of AI-generated Python code contains security weaknesses (SQL injection, XSS, auth bypass)
40% higher secret leakage rate in Copilot-using repositories (6.4% leakage rate)
CamoLeak vulnerability (CVSS 9.6) allowed prompt injection attacks to extract private source code
Code suggestions sometimes include deprecated libraries with known CVEs
Prompt injection attacks can manipulate AI suggestions to insert vulnerabilities
Shadow AI adoption growing 120% year-over-year without IT oversight
improvement_recommendations: Complete audit logging of all AI interactions for Trust Services Criteria
Block suggestions matching public code filter enabled
SAML 2.0 SSO integration with identity providers (Okta, Azure AD)
Repository-level exclusions for sensitive codebases
Secrets scanning integration (GitGuardian/TruffleHog)
Zero-training guarantee for Business/Enterprise tiers
Responsible AI Champions program to map, measure, and manage risks
NIST AI Risk Management Framework alignment (Govern, Map, Measure, Manage)
ai_audit_case_studies.reference: GitHub Trust Center, Microsoft Responsible AI Transparency Report 2025

issues_found: Voice synthesis capabilities could generate deceptive deepfakes
Multilingual performance discrepancies in speaker match classifier
Prompt injection vulnerabilities allowing manipulation of model outputs
Potential for hallucination in scientific and medical contexts
Biological threat creation risks requiring early warning systems
Emotional attachment risks from voice mode interactions
Unauthorized voice cloning attempts during red teaming
improvement_recommendations: External red teaming by 100+ experts across multiple domains
Real-time audio monitoring to detect anomalous voice generation
Content classifiers for disallowed text and image content
Speaker match classifier to prevent unauthorized voice synthesis
Break-fix framework for deployment safety decisions
Restricted release for high-risk capabilities (voice mode)
Cross-functional safety evaluation across text, vision, and audio modalities
Continuous monitoring and incident response protocols
ai_audit_case_studies.reference: GPT-4o System Card, OpenAI Trust Portal

audit_scope: Sensitive Uses and Emerging Technologies Program Review
issues_found: AI-generated diagnostic suggestions require careful validation
Patient data privacy risks in AI-generated draft replies
Potential for over-reliance on AI in clinical decision-making
Hallucination risks in medical question answering
Integration risks with existing EHR systems (Epic)
improvement_recommendations: Pre-deployment review requirements beyond standard Responsible AI Standard
Hands-on counseling for high-impact healthcare AI uses
Strict data handling protocols for PHI (Protected Health Information)
Clinical validation with medical experts before deployment
Human-in-the-loop requirements for critical diagnoses
Continuous monitoring for accuracy and safety metrics
Regular safety assessments by professional healthcare audit firms

issues_found: Human reviewers may access enterprise conversations for AI improvement
File-sharing permissions may expose sensitive data to Gemini
Cross-border data transfer concerns for GDPR compliance
Do not enter anything you would not want a human reviewer to see
Screenshot protection and data leakage prevention required
improvement_recommendations: Granular access settings for devices and OS in Admin console
PII masking and screenshot protection enabled
Complete activity logging for audit trails
Restrict user access levels to specific organizational units
Audit Google Drive permissions before Gemini rollout
Simulate real-world scenarios to test sensitive data handling
Implement enterprise-grade data loss prevention (DLP) controls

issues_found: Three separate model collections released (Phi-3, Phi-4, Phi-4-multimodal)
Each release unlocks new capabilities requiring fresh safety assessment
Smaller models may have different vulnerability profiles than large models
Educational and research use cases require specific safety considerations
improvement_recommendations: Break-fix framework: identify vulnerabilities, fix them, then redeploy
Iterative safety assessment for each model collection release
Risk management approach reviewed by Responsible AI Community experts
Documentation of safety mitigations for each capability unlock
Pre-deployment oversight with centralized workflow tools
Recommendations grounded in Responsible AI Standard policies
